title: Buttery Chewy Malaysian Potato Starch Pancake
description: This gluten-free dish combines the chewiness of potato starch with a buttery flavor for a delightful brunch option that fits the Malaysian culinary style.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine potato starch and almond flour. If using cocoa powder, add it now.
Add melted coconut oil, milk, baking powder, vanilla extract, and salt to the dry ingredients. Stir until well combined.
Fold in any additional ingredients like chocolate chips or nuts for added flavor if desired.
Using a cookie scoop or spoon, drop batter onto preheated skillet lined with parchment paper or cooking spray.
Cook pancakes on medium heat (350°F) for about 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Brush lightly with unsalted butter during the last minute of cooking if desired. Place cooked pancakes on a plate and serve warm.
Serve with your favorite spread such as honey or coconut cream for an extra indulgent touch.
